page.html:
KLF GroupCharacteristicsKLF Family MembersFunction
Group 1Presence of CtBP- binding sitesKLFs 3, 8 and 12Transcriptional repressors through their interaction with the carboxy-terminal binding protein (CtBP)
Group 2Ability to bind deacetylasesKLFs 1, 2, 4, 5, 6 and 7Transcriptional activators
Group 3Presence of a Sin3A-binding sitesKLFs 9, 10, 11, 13, 14 and 16Repressor activity through their interaction with the common transcriptional co-repressor Sin3A

cells.cell_text: Transcriptional repressors through their interaction with the carboxy-terminal binding protein (CtBP)
Repressor activity through their interaction with the common transcriptional co-repressor Sin3A

page.content: The data presented in the table offers a comprehensive overview of the characteristics and functional attributes of various KLF (Krüppel-like factor) family members, particularly focusing on their role as transcriptional repressors. The "Clusters" column categorizes the KLFs based on their shared characteristics, while the "Sequences/Cluster" metric indicates the average number of unique sequences associated with each cluster. Notably, the analysis identifies Group 1 KLFs, which include KLFs 3, 8, and 12, that are characterized by the presence of CtBP-binding sites. This binding capacity suggests a significant regulatory role in transcriptional repression through their interaction with the carboxy-terminal binding protein (CtBP). The reported data also reflects a total of 106 identified sequences associated with these KLFs, with a calculated average of 1.48 sequences per cluster, highlighting the diverse regulatory mechanisms employed by this group. Such insights are pivotal for understanding the molecular functions of KLFs and their implications in cellular processes, including development and disease.
